zh.lifeSummary: Isabel de Ros是Mary Longespée的女兒，於大約1225年出生在英國諾圖波藍德瓦可。她於大約1240年與Robert de Neville 1st Lord Raby在英國英格蘭達勒姆拉比結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和1個女兒的父母。她去世於1273年4月13日在英國達勒姆拉比 卡素享年49歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭達勒姆斯泰德洛普。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Isabel de Ros是Mary Longespée的女儿，于大约1225年出生在英国諾圖波藍德瓦可。她于大约1240年与Robert de Neville 1st Lord Raby在英国英格兰達勒姆拉比结婚。他们至少是4个儿子和1个女儿的父母。她去世于1273年4月13日在英国達勒姆拉比 卡素享年49岁，埋葬在英国英格兰達勒姆斯泰德洛普。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Ros
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Enrique, Luis, Jose, Alberto, Eduardo, Rafael, Elena, Fidel, Joaquin, Manuel, Pablo, Ramon.  Southeast Asian Leang, Phon, Lap, Phan, Pho, Saroeun, Sophal, Sophan, Sophea, Tha, Thang, Yen.
Catalan: nickname for someone with blond hair or a fair complexion, ultimately from Latin russus.
Swedish: ornamental name from ros ‘rose’.

variants.name: Rus
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Romanian Simion, Grigore, Doina, Dorel, Ilie, Liviu, Petru, Vasile.

Czech, Slovak, and Romanian (of Slavic origin): ethnic name for a Russian, ultimately derived from an Old Norse term meaning ‘oarsman, rower’ (from the root roths-), a reference to the fact that the Russian state was established by Scandinavians who rowed their ships up rivers inland from the Baltic sea. In some cases this may also be origin of the Slovenian and Croatian surname (see 3 below).
Czech: descriptive nickname from the adjective rusý ‘blond’.

variants.name: Ross
meanings: Scottish: habitational name from one or other of a number of Scottish and English places called Ross or Roos(e), especially Roose (Lancashire) and Roos (East Yorkshire). The placenames derive from a British ancestor of Welsh rhos ‘moor, heath, plain’, which is the sense of the cognate Gaelic word ros. The Scottish surname has at least three origins. First, the Anglo-Norman family from Roos (East Yorkshire) was introduced to Scotland when Robert of Roos, lord of Wark Castle (Northumberland), married Isabella, an illegitimate daughter of King William the Lion. Second, various families took the name from the province of Ross in northern Scotland and other places of that name. Third, there were Norman families in Scotland by the 13th century who probably derived their name from Rots in Normandy (see 2 below). The descendants of Godfrey de Ros, tenant of the de Morville lords of Cunninghame, were major landholders in Ayrshire, and almost certainly took their name from Rots. The Rose family of Kilravock (Nairnshire) may take their name from either of these three (see Rose ). The lairds of Balnagown adopted the surname Ross after the earldom of Ross (to which they considered themselves rightful heirs) had passed into other hands through the female line.

English (of Norman origin): habitational name from Rots in Calvados (France), probably named with the ancient Germanic element rod ‘clearing’ (compare Rhodes ). This was the original home of a family de Ros, who were established in Kent in 1130 and had major estates in Kent until well into the 13th century.

English: habitational name from Wrose, in Shipley, near Bradford (Yorkshire), with re-spelling of Wr- as R- due to the loss of /w/ before /r/ in early modern English pronunciation. The spelling Wrose is no longer current. The placename derives from Old English wrāse ‘knot, something twisted’, referring to the steep-sided hill on which the settlement stands, with the sense ‘broken or twisting hill’.

nameMeanings.name: Isabel
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a Spanish version of   Elizabeth  , which was coined by deletion of the first syllable and alteration of the final consonant sound to one that can normally end a word in Spanish. The name was imported into France in the early Middle Ages, and thence into England. It was a royal name, and its enduring popularity may have been enhanced by the fact that it was borne by a queen of England—  Isabella  (  1296–1358 ), daughter of  Philip IV  of France—even though she led a turbulent life and eventually had her husband,  Edward II , murdered.

variants.name: Isabell
meanings: Originally a Spanish version of   Elizabeth  , which was coined by deletion of the first syllable and alteration of the final consonant sound to one that can normally end a word in Spanish. The name was imported into France in the early Middle Ages, and thence into England. It was a royal name, and its enduring popularity may have been enhanced by the fact that it was borne by a queen of England—  Isabella  (  1296–1358 ), daughter of  Philip IV  of France—even though she led a turbulent life and eventually had her husband,  Edward II , murdered.

variants.name: Isabelle
meanings: form of   Isabel  , now in frequent use in the English-speaking world.

historicEvents.title: 100 Years of Conflict Between England and France Ends
historicEvents.description: Treaty of Paris was signed on December 4, 1259, which ended 100 years of conflict between England and France.

en.lifeSummary: Isabel de Ros was born about 1225, in Wark, Northumberland, England as the daughter of Mary Longespée. She married Robert de Neville 1st Lord Raby about 1240, in Raby, Durham, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 1 daughter. She died on 13 April 1273, in Raby Castle, Durham, England, at the age of 49, and was buried in Staindrop, Durham, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Isabel de Ros est née environ 1225, à Wark, Northumberland, Angleterre, fille de Mary Longespée. Elle a épousé Robert de Neville 1st Lord Raby environ 1240, à Raby, Durham, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 1 fille. Elle est décédée le 13 avril 1273, à Raby Castle, Durham, Angleterre, à l’âge de 49 ans, et a été enterrée à Staindrop, Durham, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Isabel de Ros wurde um 1225 in Wark, Northumberland, England, als Tochter von Mary Longespée geboren. Sie hat um 1240 in Raby, Durham, England, United Kingdom Robert de Neville 1st Lord Raby geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Sie starb am 13. April 1273 im Alter von 49 Jahren in Raby Castle, Durham, England und wurde in Staindrop, Durham, England, United Kingdom begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Isabel de Ros è nata il circa 1225 nel Wark, Northumberland, England come figlia di Mary Longespée. Lei sposato Robert de Neville 1st Lord Raby circa 1240, nel Raby, Durham, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 4 figli e 1 figlia. Morì il 13 aprile 1273, nel Raby Castle, Durham, England, all'età di 49 anni, e fu sepolta a Staindrop, Durham, Inghilterra, Regno Unito.

ja.lifeSummary: Isabel de Rosは約1225年にイギリスノースウンバーランドワークでMary Longespéeの娘として生まれた。約1240年にイギリスイギリスダルハムラビーでRobert de Neville 1st Lord Rabyと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼女は1273年4月13日にイギリスダルハムラビー カストルで49歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスダルハムスティインドロップにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Isabel de Ros(은)는 대략 1225년 영국 노르스벌랜드 워크에서 Mary Longespée의 딸로 태어났다. 그녀는 대략 1240년 영국 잉글랜드 뒤람 라비에서 Robert de Neville 1st Lord Raby(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1273년 4월 13일 영국 뒤람 라비 카스틀에서 49세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 뒤람 스테인드럽에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Isabel de Ros nasceu aproximadamente 1225, em Wark, Northumberland, Inglaterra filha de Mary Longespée. Ela casou-se com Robert de Neville 1st Lord Raby aproximadamente 1240, em Raby, Durham, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhos e 1 filha. Ela faleceu em 13 de abril de 1273, em Raby Castle, Durham, Inglaterra, com 49 anos, e foi sepultada em Staindrop, Durham, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.lifeSummary: Isabel de Ros родилась около 1225 г. в местности варк, нортумбирланд, Англия. Её мать звали Mary Longespée. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Robert de Neville 1st Lord Raby около 1240 г. в местности раби, дурам, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 1 дочь. Она умерла 13 апреля 1273 г. в местности раби кастл, дурам, Англия в возрасте 49 лет и была похоронена в местности стеиндроп, дурам, Англия, Великобритания.

es.lifeSummary: Isabel de Ros nació aproximadamente 1225, en Wark, Northumberland, Inglaterra como hija de Mary Longespée. Se casó con Robert de Neville 1st Lord Raby aproximadamente 1240, en Raby, Durham, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 4 hijos y 1 hija. Murió el 13 de abril de 1273, en Raby Castle, Durham, Inglaterra, a la edad de 49 años, y fue sepultada en Staindrop, Durham, Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
